author: @Spinningininfinity
text: You've pulled🥰

author: @charlessmyth
text: I know this works, yet why does the expansion of the metal not make the hole in the center smaller.

author: @Teknopottu
text: This trick also works with stuck car wheel. Saved my day couple of weeks ago. Always remember not to heat anything you're not supposed to, say, valve or rubber.

author: @____________________________.x
text: I tried that on my bike’s rear cog once. Don’t do this, as the cog spacers are plastic and not metal as I’d assumed, lol

author: @CairnOfDunnCroftPermaculture
text: I'm curious about what you'll use the compressor bit itself for. I'd love to see a compressor powered by a wind turbine!

author: @mike289homebuilt5
text: If you have a Furnace Blower fan they get very stuck on.   Taping with a hammer causes motor problems.   A puller works easy with bit of heat.

author: @WileHeCoyote
text: HAHA! TRICKS OF THE TRADE! ;)

author: @ryanlebeck259
text:
That process in reverse could yield a joint system that holds under extreme operation and will last well until it heats up enough to slide off again.

Well used science sir, well used indeed.

author: @MS-bh7ok
text: i m really curious..what does FWG stand for and signifies?

author: @Kiyarose3999
text: It was only Ali, not a heavy thick lump of Iron, easy to remove with a bit of heat, which is the obvious solution. Especially as a pulley remover would likely break the pulley before it removes it, being made of Ali as it is.

author: @PatGilliland
text: Mad Bastard. ;)

author: @nattsurfaren
text: Please show us how to do it with explosives!

author: @pinballrobbie
text: Okey dokey.

author: @edkoetsier339
text: I use a $20 bearing puller (the one with 3 arms and hooks), sometimes with a bit of heat. I hate marring the surface of machines.

author: @stevetobias4890
text: One of the best ways to remove stubborn pulleys, nuts and the like. I have a very old tow ball I need to remove, the nut is rusted on and needs more than WD-40 and heat.

author: @mikevickers4727
text: Or an hydraulic hand ram from behind to push on the pulley

author: @chuxxsss
text: Heating then freezing can help for bearings too.

author: @mik71
text: I was hoping you were going to pull out your hemp battery powered sonic screwdriver but I suppose when you hate something that much you just got to give it the flaming bash!

author: @MrAnderson4509
text: I'll add this to prevent hart ache, if you're heating up a pulley made of cast-iron, and you have heated long enough to start having the fireworks or shooting stars leaping out of the casting of the fire you've gone too far in heating it, you're boiling it and you will cause it to fail later.

author: @chrishayes5755
text: its great you have your son to help you on projects, good team!

author: @adnacraigo6590
text: If heat doesn't do it for you then you have a real problem.

author: @jasongordon9811
text: There's nothing more satisfying than freeing a stuk part. I yell I win when it comes off.

author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
text: I almost typed use a "hot wrench" without watching. Glad to see you found one in your toolbox.

author: @matthewellisor5835
text:
Burning off the crud and ~.001"/inch diameter/ 100°F
Also works the other way'round.
Got a bushing or bearing needing interference fit into some hole, leave it in the freezer or even better on a block of dry ice to shrink down for an easy installation.

author: @SlyerFox666
text: I think someone's robbed your wheels off the transit 🤣

author: @htmagic
text: RMS, great tip! And I see your gasifier over there. When are we going to see another video on that thing working?

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text: as far as I know  .... it's the only method.

author: @stobbssee
text: For you Robert, making a induction heater would be an interesting project.

replies.author: @hanslepoeter5167
replies.text: A microwave transform coil is probably good enough. Power it, put in on there and it will creat a lot of heat in the aluminium. Only problem is that you cannot leave out the axis. I think. But you could cool that with some water maybe

author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
text: That was very interesting. I remember watching some similar when will stelter tried fix his power hammer on Alec Steele channel.  The big heavy flywheel got stuck. Propane and pullers.  Pretty neat to see all you need was a touch and crow bar. I’ve a mind to rescue a few off the washing machine drums after seeing that.

author: @TopCat2021
text: The good Ole heat wrench, one of the best ways to remove stubborn items.

author: @William_Hada
text: This is definitely a useful method! Thanks for sharing.  Heat also works great on stubborn nuts and bolts.

author: @wongowonga
text: Good tip, thanks. I must remember that for the mother in law. Blowtorch and large crowbar.... or is that technique strictly limited to mechanics?

author: @terryendicott2939
text: Alternative method:  Put about 1/2 stick of dynamite behind the pulley.

author: @markirish7599
text: Best wishes from Ireland 🇮🇪and thank you for great videos
